page: OVHcloud c2-60 is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Core Processor based on the Haswell architecture running at 3.0 GHz. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs with a single thread per core, ensuring no resource sharing at the CPU level. The hardware configuration includes 60.0 GB of RAM, providing 3.75 GB of memory per core, alongside a bundled 400 GB local SSD and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. The instance operates on the KVM hypervisor and includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks place the c2-60 in the average tier across most standard workloads. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1228.0 and a multi-core score of 9363.0, with a Passmark CPU score of 24191.73. In data processing, it handles Redis SET operations at 25.27 million operations per second and delivers static web serving throughput of 16.25 GB/sec. However, its performance in large language model text generation is weak, yielding 49.64 tokens per second on a small 135M model and dropping to 6.54 tokens per second on a medium 7B model.

The dedicated core allocation ensures consistent CPU performance without resource contention, making it a cost-efficient option for compute-bound tasks that do not require modern instruction sets like AVX-512. The lack of hyperthreading and the older Haswell architecture represent a tradeoff, as reflected in the weak LLM text generation speeds. This server is best suited for software compilation, ray tracing, web serving, database operations, and general-purpose multi-threaded applications.
description: OVHcloud c2-60 is a compute-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs on an x86_64 Intel Haswell architecture running at 3.0 GHz. Lacking hyperthreading, it allocates a dedicated thread per core with 60.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 400 GB local SSD. Operating on a KVM hypervisor with 1 Gbps network bandwidth, the instance delivers average performance across standard benchmarks, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 9363.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 24191.73. While it handles Redis SET operations at 25.27 million operations per second and static web serving at 16.25 GB/sec, its LLM text generation performance is weak. This configuration offers a stable, non-shared resource profile, making it highly suitable for software compilation, database operations, web serving, and parallel processing workloads that do not require modern AVX-512 instructions.
og_description: OVHcloud c2-60 is a compute-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated Intel cores, 60 GB RAM, and a 400 GB SSD, delivering stable performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud c2-60 is a compute-optimized server with 16 dedicated Intel cores, 60 GB RAM, and a 400 GB SSD, built for web serving and database tasks.
tagline: A dedicated 16-core compute instance with bundled SSD storage, optimized for stable multi-threaded processing and database workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated physical Intel cores with no hyperthreading for consistent compute performance
60.0 GB of system memory paired with a bundled 400 GB local SSD
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and one complimentary public IPv4 address
Average performance in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations
Weak performance for large language model text generation tasks
Well-suited for static web serving, CI/CD compilation, and parallel processing
